def _post_reply_orig_protect(chat_id, text):
    subprocess.run([SENDER, text, chat_id], timeout=60, capture_output=True, text=True)
    # Log every outbound so local_chat_history can show the bot its own past replies
    try:
        with open("/home/azureuser/.mskai-bot/outbound.jsonl", "a") as _f:
            _f.write(json.dumps({
                "ts": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
                "chat_id": chat_id,
                "text": (text or "")[:2000],
            }) + "\n")
    except Exception: pass


def post_reply(chat_id, text):  # BOT_PROTECTIONS_MSKAI_V1 — redact known passwords first
    return _post_reply_orig_protect(chat_id, _redact_passwords_protect(text))
# MSKAI_NOTRUNC_PATCH_V1 - split long replies into safe Teams chunks
def _split_for_teams(text, max_chunk=24000):
    if len(text) <= max_chunk:
        return [text]
    chunks = []
    remaining = text
    NL2 = chr(10) + chr(10)
    NL = chr(10)
    while len(remaining) > max_chunk:
        split = remaining.rfind(NL2, 0, max_chunk)
        if split < max_chunk // 2:
            split = remaining.rfind(NL, 0, max_chunk)
        if split < max_chunk // 2:
            split = remaining.rfind('. ', 0, max_chunk)
        if split < max_chunk // 2:
            split = max_chunk
        chunks.append(remaining[:split].rstrip())
        remaining = remaining[split:].lstrip()
    if remaining:
        chunks.append(remaining)
    return chunks

def post_reply_chunked(chat_id, text):
    chunks = _split_for_teams(text)
    n = len(chunks)
    for i, c in enumerate(chunks, start=1):
        body = c if n == 1 else '**(part ' + str(i) + '/' + str(n) + ')**' + chr(10) + chr(10) + c
        post_reply(chat_id, body)
        if i < n:
            time.sleep(0.5)
